title: Generic issue & label validation engine (JSON-Schema-based), with SDD as first application
description:
# Generic Issue & Label Validation Engine

A configurable, declarative validation engine for issues and labels, built on
JSON Schema. Structural validation is driven by user-authored rules in
`.jit/rules.toml`; SDD (spec-driven development) is the first application,
shipped as example rules + schemas. The engine is deliberately general: jit
becomes a flexible substrate for many methodologies (SDD, RFC processes,
compliance checklists), not an SDD-specific tool.

## Motivation

Today jit's "plan" is unstructured free text in `Issue.description`, and
validation is a handful of hard-coded checks (orphan leaves, type-label
requirement, strategic labels) in `validation.rs`/`validate.rs`. Research into
five SDD tools (OpenSpec, cc-sdd, get-shit-done, graphify, ruflo) converged on
one lever: make plans falsifiable, structured, and traceable. Rather than bolt
on SDD features, we add one generic constraint layer and express SDD as config.

## Design Summary (decision record)

The authoritative, detailed decision record lives in the linked spec document.
Headlines:

- **Rules vs gates split.** Rules are stateless, structural, evaluated on
  write or on demand. Gates are stateful, transition-blocking, often
  async/external. A gate may invoke `jit validate`, but they are distinct
  layers.
- **Rule = selector + assertion + severity.** Selectors match on a full
  matrix (type, label, state, doc-type), AND-combinable. "Profiles" are not a
  new concept: a `profile:sdd` label is just a label rules select on; all
  matching rules union.
- **Two scopes.** Issue-local rules (pure, run on write); graph/aggregate
  rules (coverage, reference-integrity, dependency-shape) run only in
  `jit validate` and gate checkers, never on write.
- **Formalism: JSON Schema.** `jsonschema` crate validates; `schemars`
  (already a dep) generates. Shorthand kinds (`require-label`,
  `require-section`, `require-doc-type`) desugar to JSON Schema for ergonomics.
  Long tail -> checker-command escape hatch (reuses gate machinery). Future
  cross-field logic -> `x-jit-*` custom keywords (extension point), optionally
  embedding CEL inside a keyword. No second formalism in v1.
- **Validation target: enriched projection.** Issues are normalized before
  validation: labels grouped by namespace + content fields parsed into one
  canonical structure by per-format parsers (Markdown via pulldown-cmark in
  v1; HTML/XML behind the same trait). Format specifics live in schemas.
- **Severity:** off / warn / error. Per-rule `enforce` flag, defaulting to
  enforce (blocking) on create/update; `--force` bypasses.
- **Storage:** `.jit/rules.toml` (rules) + `.jit/schemas/` (schemas). Nothing
  hard-coded; `jit init` scaffolds defaults reproducing today's checks.
- **CLI:** extend `jit validate [<id>] [--json]`. No `jit rule` subcommands.
- **Presets:** docs/examples only. SDD ships as example rules+schemas.
- **Dropped:** delta-specs (specs edited in place). **Deferred:** CEL as a
  top-level type, custom keywords (extension point only).

## Scope

In scope: generic validation engine (projection, JSON Schema, shorthand kinds,
graph rules), per-format parsers (md/html/xml), `jit init` default ruleset,
`jit validate` + gate wiring, migration for existing repos, SDD example
rules+schemas and documentation.

## Success Criteria

- [hard] Rules load from `.jit/rules.toml`; schemas load inline or from `.jit/schemas/*.json`.
- [hard] Selector matrix (type, label, state, doc-type) matches issues; multiple matching rules union.
- [hard] Issue-local rules evaluate on `create`/`update`; `enforce` rules block (default), `--force` bypasses; warn/off behave accordingly.
- [hard] Graph rules (coverage, reference-integrity, dependency-shape) run in `jit validate` and gate checkers only, never on write.
- [hard] Validation runs against the enriched projection (labels-by-namespace + parsed content); Markdown, HTML, and XML parsers feed one canonical structure behind a shared trait.
- [hard] JSON Schema validation via the `jsonschema` crate; shorthand kinds (`require-label`, `require-section`, `require-doc-type`) desugar to JSON Schema.
- [hard] The three existing checks (orphan leaves, type-label requirement, strategic labels) are re-expressed as default rules with identical behavior; legacy `[validation]` keys still work.
- [hard] `jit init` scaffolds a default `.jit/rules.toml`; existing repos have a one-time path to obtain defaults without silently losing checks.
- [hard] `jit validate [<id>] [--json]` reports findings with rule name, severity, and message; gates can invoke it via `--checker-command`.
- [hard] SDD ships as example `.jit/rules.toml` + schemas + docs demonstrating req/satisfies coverage and the `[hard]`/`[aspirational]` content check.
- [hard] `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets` clean; `cargo fmt` clean; tests cover engine, projection, parsers, desugaring, and migration.
- [aspirational] An `x-jit-*` custom-keyword extension point is demonstrated (even if no keyword ships enabled).
- [aspirational] Documentation positions the engine as methodology-agnostic with at least one non-SDD example.

## Non-goals

- Delta-spec authoring / merge-on-Done (dropped).
- CEL as a top-level rule type (deferred; checker-command covers the gap).
- A preset subsystem (SDD is docs/examples).
- Deep per-format semantic adapters (one generic projection + JSON Schema instead).
- Embedding validation elicitation in the CLI (stays in skills like jit-breakdown).
